jokes.text: God, I just hope there are no kids on that plane. 500 bucks they find a dead kid...
jokes.setup: Father expressing concern about a plane crash
jokes.punchline: Making a casual bet on finding dead children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes Michael's callous nature while maintaining plausible deniability of concern

jokes.text: Smartphone, stupid man.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he filmed himself by mistake
jokes.punchline: Rhyming wordplay contrasting technology with user error
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that's competent but predictable

jokes.text: But George Michael was proving more adept at lying than anyone suspected.
jokes.setup: George Michael has always been portrayed as honest and innocent
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals he's actually becoming skilled at deception
jokes.explanation: Great character development humor that subverts expectations about George Michael's nature

jokes.text: At least he wasn't drunk and bitching about Don Ameche not knowing his lines in Cocoon.
jokes.setup: Discussion about difficult fathers
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and bizarre example of paternal complaint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity that creates humor through absurdly detailed mundane complaint

jokes.text: But now, I wouldn't even think of my son as my brother. Although, in fairness, this is something a Bluth brother would do.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on how different he and George Michael are
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by noting George Michael's behavior is typically Bluth
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness and contradiction that reveals character blindness

jokes.text: It's George Michael's Arrested Development.
jokes.setup: Traditional show title card moment
jokes.punchline: Changing the show's title to reflect George Michael's focus this season
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke that plays with show structure and character focus

jokes.text: That's it for me. You guys keep playing. Oh, too funny.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to be tough and play with 'big boys'
jokes.punchline: Immediately quitting after getting hurt but pretending it's amusing
jokes.explanation: Good character-based physical comedy showing George Michael's attempt at toughness failing

jokes.text: Who would you like us to call in case of an emergency? My dad.
jokes.setup: George Michael at health center after getting injured playing tough
jokes.punchline: Still depending on his father despite trying to assert independence
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment showing the gap between George Michael's aspirations and reality

jokes.text: Well, you know older guys... the night ends for them at 8:00.
jokes.setup: George Michael lying about why his dinner with his dad ended
jokes.punchline: Dismissing his father as old when George Michael is only 22
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Michael's newfound cockiness and generational arrogance

jokes.text: And I've already got some birth control in me that I paid for myself, and I'd hate to let it go to waste.
jokes.setup: Rebel explaining why she's available
jokes.punchline: Treating birth control like a sunk cost investment
jokes.explanation: Funny economic approach to contraception that's both practical and crass

jokes.text: family first, unless there's a work thing, and then work first.
jokes.setup: George Michael quoting his father's priorities
jokes.punchline: Completely undermining the 'family first' principle with an immediate exception
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about modern priorities and parental hypocrisy

jokes.text: I have said that, too.
jokes.setup: George Michael quotes Michael's hypocritical family motto
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of his own contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan admission of guilt that shows Michael's self-awareness without shame

jokes.text: Okay, first you 'F' me with Fakeblock
jokes.setup: P-Hound expressing anger about being betrayed
jokes.punchline: Using 'F' as euphemism while discussing something called 'Fakeblock'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Not against the rules. Still a little weird, don't you think? But not against the rules.
jokes.setup: Discussion about P-Hound taking creepy photos of Maeby
jokes.punchline: P-Hound's repeated insistence that legal doesn't mean appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good character bit showing P-Hound's legalistic but creepy mindset

jokes.text: This is no longer a peace pizza. That is a war pizza.
jokes.setup: P-Hound brought pizza to make peace
jokes.punchline: Dramatically redefining the pizza's purpose due to conflict
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation that treats pizza like a diplomatic tool

jokes.text: Well, I suppose anything goes in a dorm.
jokes.setup: Rebel seeing George Michael's dorm room
jokes.punchline: Suggesting dorms are places of sexual freedom
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual setup line that's functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Well, I aim to not please.
jokes.setup: Rebel complimenting George Michael for not trying to impress
jokes.punchline: Twisting 'aim to please' into its opposite
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows George Michael's attempts at being cool through reverse psychology

jokes.text: Oh, my God, they sound like terrible people. Oh, they were.
jokes.setup: Rebel describes dinner with politician and hooker
jokes.punchline: George Michael's earnest moral judgment followed by Rebel's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between George Michael's innocence and Rebel's worldliness

jokes.text: If she were to learn that he was merely a wood block player with a heightened ability to track the passage of time, perhaps she would rightly feel lack of pretention should be a given.
jokes.setup: George Michael's internal debate about revealing his true identity
jokes.punchline: Describing his modest talents in the most pretentious way possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant verbose description of mediocre skills that perfectly captures the show's voice

jokes.text: 24. Yes, it was, uh, right in here.
jokes.setup: George Michael's internal countdown while deciding whether to lie
jokes.punchline: Confirming the lie at exactly the moment his time-tracking predicted
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that shows his precision even in the moment of deception

jokes.text: This is like making love with the Wright brothers in their bicycle shop. I'm sure she just meant one of them.
jokes.setup: Rebel making historical/sexual comparison about innovation
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact clarification about the logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment with perfect narrator intervention on an already weird metaphor

jokes.text: Hey, how about her knowing they had a bicycle shop?
jokes.setup: Previous Wright Brothers reference
jokes.punchline: Narrator appreciating Rebel's historical knowledge rather than the sexual content
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment where the narrator gets sidetracked by intellectual appreciation

jokes.text: Action. Tweet and drive. Yeah, that was good, but it's 'text.' And we need the word 'don't.'
jokes.setup: Rebel filming a PSA about safe driving
jokes.punchline: Getting the anti-safety message wrong in multiple ways
jokes.explanation: Good satire of PSA production and celebrity involvement in causes

jokes.text: Oh, right, yeah, 'cause, uh, we're twins, and, uh, we have no sense of ourselves as individuals. That's right.
jokes.setup: George Michael suggesting twins will vote the same way
jokes.punchline: Twin sarcastically agreeing while proving the stereotype
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where the character proves the stereotype while denying it

jokes.text: The twins were proud to show that they voted differently from one another.
jokes.setup: Twins insisting they're individuals
jokes.punchline: Narrator noting their pride in basic independence
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about the low bar for twin individuality

jokes.text: I guess one head's better than two, huh? Twin hater.
jokes.setup: George Michael suggesting bringing in a single person
jokes.punchline: Twins interpreting practical suggestion as discriminatory
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on 'two heads are better than one' that escalates to manufactured outrage

jokes.text: We've been dealing with 'twincists' like you all our lives.
jokes.setup: Twins feeling discriminated against
jokes.punchline: Creating a new form of prejudice with portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that creates a believable new form of discrimination

jokes.text: Bender from Futurama. I almost wrote that.
jokes.setup: Formal vote between George Michael and P-Hound
jokes.punchline: Random cartoon character vote with twin solidarity
jokes.explanation: Random humor that works through absurdity and twin coordination

jokes.text: I happened to remember how much you love bologna.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to be thoughtful to George Michael
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong about his nephew's preferences
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's well-intentioned but misguided gestures

jokes.text: He didn't think George Michael liked bologna.
jokes.setup: Gob's confident assertion about bologna
jokes.punchline: Narrator's understated correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator timing that deflates Gob's misplaced confidence

jokes.text: Can't lie to you, George Michael, it's a forget-me-now.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking about mysterious pill
jokes.punchline: Gob immediately revealing it's a memory-erasing drug
jokes.explanation: Good callback with character-appropriate honesty from Gob

jokes.text: Take a pill to forget your problems. Take a pill to go to sleep. And take a pill to forget your problems. Need an erection, take a pill. Need to forget your problems, take a pill. Take a pill and your problems are forgotten. Just take a pill.
jokes.setup: Gob philosophizing about modern medicine
jokes.punchline: Repetitive, circular logic that goes nowhere
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate rambling that's intentionally repetitive and meandering

jokes.text: Ninja please.
jokes.setup: Discussion about money for house purchase
jokes.punchline: Gob's attempt at being cool with ninja/racial substitution
jokes.explanation: Dated humor that works mainly through Gob's misguided attempt to be hip

jokes.text: 22? You look 16. He's 22! He's 22, but looks 16.
jokes.setup: Sudden Valley's reputation for attracting sex offenders
jokes.punchline: Residents' inappropriate excitement about young-looking resident
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that builds on established running gag about the neighborhood

jokes.text: It's like it's everyone's birthday.
jokes.setup: George Michael receiving gifts from neighbors
jokes.punchline: Innocent interpretation of suspicious neighbor behavior
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between George Michael's innocence and the creepy reality

jokes.text: We said no clothes, Carlos. Hat is not clothes. A hat is clothes.
jokes.setup: Impromptu football game
jokes.punchline: Debate about nudity rules during sports
jokes.explanation: Absurdist moment that adds to the inappropriate neighborhood atmosphere

jokes.text: Guys, maybe... maybe we can't play skins versus skins. Tops versus bottoms?
jokes.setup: All-male nudity creating team distinction problems
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent suggestion with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Excellent innocent sexual innuendo that perfectly fits George Michael's character

jokes.text: No, this is my son, Lem Depardieu.
jokes.setup: George Michael meeting unexpected child at his party
jokes.punchline: Child's pretentious French name
jokes.explanation: Standard Hollywood pretentious name humor that's competent but predictable

jokes.text: In fact, it was the folks in the pool who were more at risk of being near Lem than he was of them.
jokes.setup: Rebel worrying about her son's safety with the neighbors
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing the child is the dangerous one
jokes.explanation: Dark humor twist that subverts expectations about child safety

jokes.text: I take things too serious. I take things too seriously. I need to note which one of those is correct, because I sound like a horse's ass when I alternate between the two of them.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining his relationship problem
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by grammar while making his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Michael's overthinking and self-consciousness

jokes.text: 'Serioush' is nothing. That's not a word.
jokes.setup: George Michael's made-up word
jokes.punchline: Maeby's blunt correction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of pretentious language invention

jokes.text: Maeby, you're fired!
jokes.setup: George Michael finally having power as CEO
jokes.punchline: Immediately firing his cousin/former crush
jokes.explanation: Satisfying character moment showing George Michael's newfound assertiveness

jokes.text: So... you're the other guy.
jokes.setup: Michael mentioning he's dating Rebel
jokes.punchline: Maeby realizing the family connection
jokes.explanation: Great plot revelation moment that sets up the central conflict

jokes.text: She wants it easily-breezily
jokes.setup: Maeby describing what Rebel wants in a relationship
jokes.punchline: The made-up phrase 'easily-breezily' as relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Maeby's characteristic way of speaking with made-up casual expressions

jokes.text: I want you to know that I think that I've been coming on a little bit too strong... and we're walking down the aisle
jokes.setup: Michael trying to play it cool and casual
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalating to marriage talk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Bluth contradiction showing his inability to actually be casual

jokes.text: Was that fun for you? No.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about their sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Rebel's blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Brutal comedic timing with perfect economy of language

jokes.text: Are you gonna call me? If I'm in traffic.
jokes.setup: Post-coital conversation about future contact
jokes.punchline: Reference to their lie tag game from the opening
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Michael reverting to his deceptive pattern

jokes.text: ♪ I just stole this fair young miss ♪ ♪ From a big shot, George Mahar... ♪
jokes.setup: Michael feeling triumphant about his relationship
jokes.punchline: Singing about stealing from his own son without knowing it
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony with Michael celebrating unknowingly hurting his son

jokes.text: You're out of the movie
jokes.setup: Michael discovers he's been set up by Maeby
jokes.punchline: He immediately fires her from the movie project
jokes.explanation: Michael's immediate revenge is character-appropriate. Quick escalation from discovery to action.

jokes.text: I mean, who would not want to raise their kids here?
jokes.setup: George Michael praising Sudden Valley as a family community
jokes.punchline: Question about raising children in a sex offender haven
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark irony showing George Michael's dangerous naivety

jokes.text: Oh, great... pain fades slowly. Quickly.
jokes.setup: George Michael saying his relationship might be over
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate correction showing his distraction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's conflicted feelings about George Michael's pain

jokes.text: I would rather have sex with someone because they thought I was someone else than not have sex with someone because they thought I was me.
jokes.setup: George Michael weighing his relationship options
jokes.punchline: Profound statement about self-worth and sexual identity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight that's both funny and genuinely moving about self-esteem

jokes.text: Well, I figured who better than the Chinese to build a wall. But, no, I couldn't afford them, so I... I hired, um, they're technically Mongols, I guess.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his wall-building contractors
jokes.punchline: Hiring the people the Great Wall was built to keep out
jokes.explanation: Excellent historical irony with perfect Gob logic and escalating absurdity

jokes.text: A horde. That's the minimum. They don't come in anything less than a horde.
jokes.setup: Michael asking how many Mongols Gob hired
jokes.punchline: Treating 'horde' as a unit of measurement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay treating historical terminology as modern business practice

jokes.text: they're gonna have a major Mongolian beef with us
jokes.setup: Explaining consequences of not paying the Mongols
jokes.punchline: Pun on the Chinese dish 'Mongolian beef'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works within the absurd context Gob has created

jokes.text: Yeah, so, I'm George Maharis, and, uh... and Fakeblock is real... Let's put up this wall
jokes.setup: George Michael finally deciding to tell the truth about his identity
jokes.punchline: Instead doubling down on the lies and supporting the wall
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where George Michael chooses deception over truth at the crucial moment

jokes.text: So there better be two Rebels in here, am I right?
jokes.setup: Michael discovering George Michael is dating Rebel
jokes.punchline: Desperate hope for impossible coincidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment showing Michael's denial and desperation

jokes.text: How'd you know about the photo booth?
jokes.setup: Michael mentioning photo booth detail
jokes.punchline: George Michael realizing Michael has inside knowledge
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot revelation that confirms both characters' worst fears

jokes.text: But like the victim of a roofie circle, having taken one was the only memory he had lost.
jokes.setup: Michael could have explained forget-me-now use
jokes.punchline: Ironic description of memory loss being the only lost memory
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor about the irony of drug-induced amnesia

jokes.text: Hey, Gobie. It's Tony. We still on for our sex date tonight?
jokes.setup: Gob being told to avoid scandals
jokes.punchline: Immediate call about gay encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for maximum comedic irony about avoiding scandals

jokes.text: That left hand might be difficult to cuff. Trouble cuffing him. I'm not a monster!
jokes.setup: Police trying to arrest Buster
jokes.punchline: His missing hand making handcuffing difficult, and his protest about being a monster
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback combined with Buster's characteristic self-defense

jokes.text: Love each other
jokes.setup: News report ending about the Bluth family murder charges
jokes.punchline: John Beard ending with the same generic advice Gob gave earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback that ties together the episode's themes with the news anchor's signature sign-off
